title: The Complete Transformer Architecture
concept: Combining the encoder stack and decoder stack creates the complete sequence-to-sequence Transformer. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: LayerNorm & Residuals.'

exercises.prompt: What components are joined together to form a complete sequence-to-sequence models combine to form a complete sequence-to-sequence Transformer?

options.text: An encoder stack combined with a decoder stack

exercises.explain: A complete Transformer is built by linking an encoder stack (for input understanding) and a decoder stack (for output generation).

cards.title: Connecting the Dots
cards.body: We've looked at the reader (Encoder) and the writer (Decoder) separately. But how do they join forces to translate languages, write summaries, and chat with us? Let's connect the two halves!

cards.title: The Complete Transformer
cards.body: A complete Transformer combines two main stacks. The encoder stack reads and understands the input text all at once. The decoder stack then takes this understanding and generates a new output sequence, one step at a time.

cards.title: The Translation Desk
cards.body: Think of the complete Transformer like an international translation desk. The Encoder is the translator who listens to the original French speech and writes down the core ideas. The Decoder is the writer who takes those core ideas and drafts the speech in Spanish, word by word. Together, they get the job done smoothly!

cards.title: Language Translation
cards.body:
In Google Translate, when you translate 'Hello, how are you?' into Spanish:
1. The Encoder reads the entire English sentence to understand its meaning.
2. The Decoder uses this meaning to generate '¿Hola, cómo estás?' word by word.

cards.title: Do They Work Alone?
cards.body:
Myth: The encoder and decoder stacks are completely isolated and never talk to each other.
Fact: They are deeply connected! The decoder constantly looks back at the encoder's outputs using a special connection called 'cross-attention' to make sure it translates the right words.

cards.title: Encoder vs. Decoder Stack
cards.body: The complete Transformer links both sides together to handle input and output seamlessly.

right.text: Generates the output text step-by-step using the encoder's clues.

left.text: Reads the input text all at once to understand the context.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following tasks is best suited for a complete sequence-to-sequence Transformer?

options.text: Translating a sentence from English to French
Storing files on a hard drive
Increasing the volume of an audio file

exercises.explain: The complete Transformer architecture is designed for sequence-to-sequence tasks like translating text from one language to another.

exercises.prompt: True or False: In a complete Transformer, the decoder generates the output sequence one step at a time.
exercises.explain: The encoder reads the entire input sequence at once, but the decoder generates the output sequence step-by-step (word-by-word).

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing part of the complete Transformer architecture.
exercises.sentence: In a complete sequence-to-sequence Transformer, the encoder processes the input and the _______ generates the output.
exercises.explain: The encoder reads and analyzes the input, while the decoder uses that analysis to generate the final output sequence.

exercises.prompt: Identify the scenario that uses a complete sequence-to-sequence Transformer:

options.text: An AI chatbot reading your prompt and writing a creative poem in response.
A sensor turning on a light when it detects motion.

exercises.explain: The prompt asks the AI to write a poem. A complete sequence-to-sequence Transformer (Encoder-Decoder) takes the prompt as input, understands it, and generates the new poem as output.

exercises.prompt: How do the Encoder and Decoder stacks work together in a complete sequence-to-sequence Transformer?

options.text: The Decoder operates completely independently without using any Encoder information.
The Encoder generates the final output sequence directly without a Decoder.
The Encoder processes the input sequence, and the Decoder uses that information to generate the output sequence.
Transformers only use Encoders and do not support Decoders.

exercises.explain: The complete Transformer structure connects an encoder stack (for reading and understanding) directly to a decoder stack (for generating the new sequence). Next up: LayerNorm & Residuals.
